original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children — Recommendations
original_registry_entry.version: NICE NG75; current official recommendations page checked 2026-07-11
original_registry_entry.population: Infants and children with weight loss or concerns about faltering growth.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary, community, and specialist pediatric care.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for faltering-growth assessment and feeding history; partial for undifferentiated acute poor feeding and not directly validated for UAE practice.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.2–1.2.14
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies weight and height measurement, clinical/developmental/social assessment, detailed feeding or eating history, observation, and assessment-directed investigations.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.10–1.2.12
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies suckling, bottle feeding, routines, environment, aversion, interaction, appetite, food types, and feeding-diary domains.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.27–1.2.32
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines growth monitoring and referral for underlying disorder, failure to respond, slow growth, rapid weight loss, severe undernutrition, safeguarding concern, or acute illness.

title: Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: Infants and children with weight loss or concerns about faltering growth.
intended_setting: Primary, community, and specialist pediatric care.
